rulings.Case Title: AARON KUMLIN et al VS. BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 16, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT BETHEL CHRISTIAN CHURCH, TIM RAIMER, STEVE KORCH DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAIN WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAIN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 2ND AND 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD EMOTIONAL DISTRESS ARISING FROM ACTIVITIES OTHER THAN THE TERMINATION AND ALLEGED DEFAMATION THAT LED TO TERMINATION, WHICH ARE BARRED BY THE MINISTERIAL EXCEPTION. OVERRULED AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION. =(302/PHA)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA K LOUIE VS. ROBERT S LOUIE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notic And Motion For Appointment Of Referee To Recommend Disposition Of Property In Partition Dispute
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 16, 2011, line 4, PLAINTIFF PATRICIA LOUIE Notice And Motion For Appointment Of Referee To Recommend Disposition Of Property In Partition Dispute. DENY, MOTION UNNECESSARY AS PARTIES AGREE PROPERTY SHOULD BE SOLD. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER RE LISTING AGENT. =(302/PHA)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELICA M. GONZALEZ VS. ALFONSO PICAZO, AS TRUSTEES OF THE ARTHUR AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 16, 2011, line 5, PLAINTIFF ANGELICA GONZALEZ Notice And Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Cross-Complaint. 1ST GROUND - OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT IN LIGHT OF RULING ON DEMURRER. 3RD GROUND - GRANT WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD WHETHER ALLOWED BY CONTRACT OR STATUTE. 2ND GROUND - GRANT WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD NECESSARY FACTS. =(302/PHA)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELICA M. GONZALEZ VS. ALFONSO PICAZO, AS TRUSTEES OF THE ARTHUR AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 16, 2011, line 5. PLAINTIFF ANGELICA GONZALEZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. First cause of action sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to attach settlement agreement. Overruled on other grounds (raise factual issues). Second cause of action sustained with leave to amend (to state whether breach of fiduciary duty or negligence). =(302/PHA)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELICA M. GONZALEZ VS. ALFONSO PICAZO, AS TRUSTEES OF THE ARTHUR AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 16, 2011, line 5. PLAINTIFF ANGELICA GONZALEZ'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Grant motion for summary adjudication. Receiver J. Cantrell is appointed referee to conduct sale; referee shall seek court approval before completion of sale. =(302/PHA)

rulings.Case Title: MARCIA GIUSTI et al VS. BKCM ENTERPRISES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORAION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 16, 2011, line 6. DEFENDANTS JOHN SIMONSE, MAGNATE FUND #1 LLC, MAGNATE FUND #2 LLC, MAGNATE FUND #3 LLC, 27TH STREET ASSOCIATES LLC, 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, SOUTH VAN NESS ASSOCIATES LLC, LHJS INVESTMENTS, LLC, JWS CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to 1st cause of action (fraud), 3rd cause of action (I.I.E.D.). Sustained with leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action (conversion); 5th cause of action (constructive trust) to be filed as a remedy. Sustained without leave to amend as to 4th cause of action (declaratory relief). =(302/PHA)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 16, 2011, line 7, PLAINTIFF TERRIE DANIELS DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. OFF CALENDAR. NO PROOF OF SERVICE OR COURTESY COPIES PER SFLR 2.6B. =(302/PHA)

rulings.Case Title: DWIGHT L MERRIMAN III VS. STOCKBRIDGE CAPITAL GROUP LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 16, 2011, line 8, DEFENDANT STOCKBRIDGE CAPITAL GROUP LLC Notice of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Motion To Stay. DENY. COURT FINDS THAT (1) ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS SEPARABLE AND NOT SUBJECT TO THE CITED CONDITION PRECEDENT; (2) DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO ATTACH THE AAA RULES DEMONSTRATES THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT MEET THE CLEAR AND UNMISTAKABLE STANDARD RE AN ARBITRATION DECIDING ARBITRABILITY; (4) THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE IS UNCONSCIONABLE; AND (5) THE UNCONSCIONABLE PROVISIONS CANNOT BE SEVERED. =(302/PHA)
